changes_made: Renamed k8s_audit_logging_enabled to k8s_apiserver_audit_logging_enabled for clarity and consistency with resource naming conventions
review_notes: The control SI-4 (2) is applicable to Kubernetes as it involves real-time monitoring and analysis, which can be implemented through audit logging and admission controllers. The function names mostly follow the naming conventions, but a minor adjustment was needed for clarity and consistency. The functions map well to Kubernetes security controls, focusing on audit logging and admission controllers for real-time monitoring.
naming_improvements: Ensure resource names are specific, such as using 'apiserver' for audit logging functions
title: System Monitoring | Automated Tools and Mechanisms for Real-time Analysis
step1_reasoning: The control SI-4 (2) focuses on automated tools and mechanisms for real-time analysis of system monitoring. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to enabling audit logging for real-time monitoring of API server activities, configuring audit policies to ensure detailed and timely log capture, and using admission controllers for security monitoring and policy enforcement. These mechanisms provide the necessary infrastructure for real-time analysis and monitoring within a Kubernetes environment.
